rev: Self-paced worksheet-based Singapore Math with strong heuristics and problem-solving; broad coverage, though the self-directed format suits self-motivated kids more.
Long-established dual-hand abacus chain with the widest island footprint; effective for speed but drill-heavy.
Game-based Vedic-math approach that keeps young kids engaged through play; fun, though shortcuts diverge from the MOE syllabus.
Structured 10-level 9-bead abacus course across heartland outlets; solid progression but traditional and repetitive.
Single-location, MOE-aligned hands-on maths at SAFRA Toa Payoh; convenient there but limited reach.
Premium brand pairing heuristics with a critical-thinking pitch; polished but priced high.
Play-based early-concept maths aimed at preschoolers; gentle and exploratory, thin public detail.
Five eastern/central outlets with transparent pricing and a refundable trial; classic mental-arithmetic drilling.
Globally recognised abacus franchise with 15+ centres and a free assessment; quality can vary by branch.
Heartland-focused abacus chain with a free first session; affordable and steady, purely arithmetic.
Boutique two-outlet abacus centre in Bukit Timah and Katong; small groups but among the priciest abacus options.
Veteran, well-priced abacus school across Toa Payoh/Bishan/Novena; no-frills and traditional.
Fast-paced right-brain programme with flash-based numeracy; engaging for very young kids but expensive.
Premium right-brain method weaving in number and memory work; strong on early stimulation but pricey.
Three north-east/central outlets with mid-range fees and a small trial; reliable drilling without frills.
West-side abacus specialist with a free trial and assessment; handy for Jurong/Clementi but region-limited.
In-person and cheaper online abacus classes with a free session; flexible, but skews age 6 and up.
